name: Sorcerer
description: Sorcerers wield innate magic that flows from their very being. Unlike Wizards who study arcane texts, Sorcerers are born with or acquire power through transformative events—a dragon ancestor, a brush with the Far Realm, the touch of wild chaos. They shape that power through Metamagic, bending spells to their will with the Sorcery Points that fuel their craft.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description:
Drawing from your innate magic, you can cast spells. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Sorcerer spells.

**Cantrips.** You know four Sorcerer cantrips of your choice. {spell:Light}, {spell:Prestidigitation}, {spell:Shocking Grasp}, and {spell:Sorcerous Burst} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Sorcerer cantrip of your choice. When you reach Sorcerer levels 4 and 10, you learn another Sorcerer cantrip of your choice.

**Spell Slots.** The Sorcerer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Prepared Spells of Level 1+.** You prepare the list of level 1+ spells available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Sorcerer spells. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Sorcerer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Sorcerer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Sorcerer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. If another Sorcerer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Sorcerer spells for you.

**Changing Your Prepared Spells.** Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Sorcerer spell for which you have spell slots.

**Spellcasting Focus.** You can use an {item:Arcane Focus} as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus} for your Sorcerer spells.

features.name: Innate Sorcery
features.description:
An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with simmering magic. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits:

- The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
- You have {rule:Advantage} on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.

You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Spell save DC increases by 1, and you have advantage on Sorcerer spell attack rolls.

features.name: Font of Magic
features.description:
You can tap into the wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by Sorcery Points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

You have 2 Sorcery Points, and you gain more as you reach higher levels, as shown in the Sorcery Points column of the Sorcerer Features table. You can't have more Sorcery Points than the number shown in the table for your level. You regain all expended Sorcery Points when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Converting Spell Slots to Sorcery Points.** You can expend a spell slot to gain a number of Sorcery Points equal to the slot's level (no action required).

**Creating Spell Slots.** As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can transform unexpended Sorcery Points into one spell slot. You can create a spell slot no higher than level 5. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. Costs: level 1 = 2 points (min. Sorcerer 2), level 2 = 3 points (min. 3), level 3 = 5 points (min. 5), level 4 = 6 points (min. 7), level 5 = 7 points (min. 9).

properties.text: Sorcery Points can convert spell slots to points (slot level = points gained) or create new spell slots (Bonus Action): 2 pts = 1st-level, 3 pts = 2nd-level, 5 pts = 3rd-level, 6 pts = 4th-level, 7 pts = 5th-level. Created slots vanish on Long Rest.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description:
Because your magic flows from within, you can alter your spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice from the Metamagic Options available to Sorcerers. You use the chosen options to temporarily modify spells you cast. To use an option, you must spend the number of Sorcery Points that it costs.

You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it unless otherwise noted in one of those options. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don't know. You gain two more options at Sorcerer level 10 and two more at Sorcerer level 17.

properties.text: Choose 2 Metamagic options at level 2. Gain 2 more at level 10 (total 4), and 2 more at level 17 (total 6). Can replace one option each Sorcerer level.

features.name: Sorcerer Subclass
features.description: You gain a Sorcerer subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Sorcerer levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Sorcerer level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Sorcerer levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Sorcerous Restoration
features.description: When you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, you can regain expended Sorcery Points, but no more than a number equal to half your Sorcerer level (round down). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: On Short Rest: regain up to half your Sorcerer level (rounded down) expended Sorcery Points. Usable once per Long Rest.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Sorcery Incarnate
features.description:
If you have no uses of Innate Sorcery left, you can use it if you spend 2 Sorcery Points when you take the {rule:Bonus Action} to activate it.

In addition, while your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use up to two of your Metamagic options on each spell you cast.

properties.text: Can activate Innate Sorcery even when no uses remain by spending 2 Sorcery Points. While Innate Sorcery is active, you can apply up to two Metamagic options per spell.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You gain two additional Metamagic options of your choice, for a total of four. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don't know.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Metamagic
features.description: You gain two additional Metamagic options of your choice, for a total of six. Whenever you gain a Sorcerer level, you can replace one of your Metamagic options with one you don't know.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Sorcerer subclass.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. {feat:Boon of Dimensional Travel} is recommended.

features.name: Arcane Apotheosis
features.description: While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use one Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.

properties.text: While Innate Sorcery is active, one Metamagic option per turn costs 0 Sorcery Points.

subclasses.name: Aberrant Sorcery
subclasses.description: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you. Perhaps a psychic wind from the Astral Plane carried psionic energy to you, or you were exposed to the Far Realm's warping influence. Alternatively, you were implanted with a mind flayer tadpole, but your transformation into a mind flayer never occurred; now the tadpole's psionic power is yours.

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description:
When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Psionic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

| Sorcerer Level | Spells |
|---|---|
| 3rd | {spell:Arms of Hadar}, {spell:Calm Emotions}, {spell:Detect Thoughts}, {spell:Dissonant Whispers}, {spell:Mind Sliver} |
| 5th | {spell:Hunger of Hadar}, {spell:Sending} |
| 7th | {spell:Evard's Black Tentacles}, {spell:Summon Aberration} |
| 9th | {spell:Rary's Telepathic Bond}, {spell:Telekinesis} |

features.name: Telepathic Speech
features.description:
You can form a telepathic connection between your mind and the mind of another. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You and the chosen creature can communicate telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile). To understand each other, you each must mentally use a language the other knows.

The telepathic connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your Sorcerer level. It ends early if you use this ability to form a connection with a different creature.

properties.text: Bonus Action: establish telepathic link with one creature you can see within 30 ft. Link range = Charisma modifier miles (min. 1). Duration = Sorcerer level in minutes. Ends early if you link to a new creature. Both must share a known language.

features.name: Psionic Sorcery
features.description: When you cast any level 1+ spell from your Psionic Spells feature, you can cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of Sorcery Points equal to the spell's level. If you cast the spell using Sorcery Points, it requires no Verbal or Somatic components, and it requires no Material components unless they are consumed by the spell or have a cost specified in it.

properties.text: Psionic Spells can be cast by spending Sorcery Points equal to spell level instead of a spell slot. When cast this way, the spell needs no V, S, or M components (unless the material is consumed or has a listed cost).

features.name: Psychic Defenses
features.description: You have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to Psychic damage, and you have {rule:Advantage} on saving throws to avoid or end the {condition:Charmed} or {condition:Frightened} condition.

properties.condition: to avoid or end Charmed or Frightened

features.name: Revelation in Flesh
features.description:
You can unleash the aberrant truth hidden within yourself. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point or more to magically alter your body for 10 minutes. For each Sorcery Point you spend, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice, the effects of which last until the alteration ends.

**Aquatic Adaptation.** You gain a Swim Speed equal to twice your Speed, and you can breathe underwater.

**Glistening Flight.** You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed, and you can hover.

**See the Invisible.** You can see any {condition:Invisible} creature within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover.

**Wormlike Movement.** Your body, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, becomes slimy and pliable. You can move through any space as narrow as 1 inch, and you can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or the {condition:Grappled} condition.

properties.text: Bonus Action: spend 1+ Sorcery Points (1 per benefit) to alter your body for 10 minutes. Benefits: Swim Speed ×2 + water breathing; Fly Speed = Speed + hover; see Invisible creatures within 60 ft not in Total Cover; move through 1-inch spaces and escape nonmagical restraints/Grappled with 5 ft of movement.

features.name: Warping Implosion
features.description:
You can unleash a space-warping anomaly. As a Magic action, you teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet of yourself. Immediately after you disappear, each creature within 30 feet of the space you left must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d10 Force damage and is pulled straight toward the space you left, ending in an unoccupied space as close to your former space as possible. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Magic action: teleport up to 120 ft (unoccupied visible space). Creatures within 30 ft of origin make STR save (DC = spell save DC): fail = 3d10 Force damage and pulled to origin; success = half damage. Restore use by spending 5 Sorcery Points.

subclasses.name: Clockwork Sorcery
subclasses.description: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency. You or someone from your lineage might have become entangled in the machinations of modrons, the orderly beings who inhabit Mechanus. Whatever its origin within you, the power of order can seem strange to others, but for you, it's part of a vast and glorious system.

features.name: Clockwork Spells
features.description:
When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Clockwork Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

| Sorcerer Level | Spells |
|---|---|
| 3rd | {spell:Aid}, {spell:Alarm}, {spell:Lesser Restoration}, {spell:Protection from Evil and Good} |
| 5th | {spell:Dispel Magic}, {spell:Protection from Energy} |
| 7th | {spell:Freedom of Movement}, {spell:Summon Construct} |
| 9th | {spell:Greater Restoration}, {spell:Wall of Force} |

In addition, choose or randomly determine a manifestation of your connection to order that appears while you cast any of your Sorcerer spells (for example: spectral cogwheels hovering behind you, clock hands spinning in your eyes, or ticking gears audible to those nearby).

features.name: Restore Balance
features.description:
Your connection to the plane of absolute order allows you to equalize chaotic moments. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself is about to roll a d20 with {rule:Advantage} or {rule:Disadvantage}, you can take a {rule:Reaction} to prevent the roll from being affected by Advantage and Disadvantage.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Reaction: when a creature you can see within 60 ft is about to roll a d20 with Advantage or Disadvantage, cancel both — the roll is made normally.

features.name: Bastion of Law
features.description:
You can tap into the grand equation of existence to imbue a creature with a shimmering shield of order. As a Magic action, you can expend 1 to 5 Sorcery Points to create a magical ward around yourself or another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of Sorcery Points spent to create it. When the warded creature takes damage, it can expend a number of those dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled on those dice.

The ward lasts until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} or until you use this feature again.

properties.text: Magic action: spend 1–5 Sorcery Points to create a ward on yourself or a creature within 30 ft. Ward = that many d8s. When warded creature takes damage, it can roll any number of remaining ward dice and subtract the total from damage. Ward lasts until Long Rest or next use.

features.name: Trance of Order
features.description:
You gain the ability to align your consciousness with the endless calculations of Mechanus. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can enter this state for 1 minute. For the duration, attack rolls against you can't benefit from {rule:Advantage}, and whenever you make a {rule:D20 Test}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Bonus Action: enter trance for 1 minute. During trance: attack rolls against you can't have Advantage; on any D20 Test you make, treat d20 rolls of 1–9 as a 10. Restore use by spending 5 Sorcery Points.

features.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
features.description:
You momentarily summon spirits of order to expunge disorder around you. As a Magic action, you summon the spirits in a 30-foot Cube originating from you. The spirits look like modrons or other Constructs of your choice. They are intangible and invulnerable, and they create the following effects within the Cube before vanishing.

**Heal.** The spirits restore up to 100 Hit Points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures of your choice in the Cube.

**Repair.** Any damaged objects entirely in the Cube are repaired instantly.

**Dispel.** Every spell of level 6 and lower ends on creatures and objects of your choice in the Cube.

Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you spend 7 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Magic action: summon spirits in 30-ft Cube. Effects: restore up to 100 HP split among chosen creatures; repair all damaged objects; end all spells level 6 and lower on chosen targets. Restore use by spending 7 Sorcery Points.

subclasses.name: Draconic Sorcery
subclasses.description: Your innate magic comes from the gift of a dragon. Perhaps an ancient dragon facing death bequeathed some of its magical power to you or your ancestor. You might have absorbed magic from a site infused with dragons' power, handled treasure from a dragon's hoard steeped in draconic power, or you have a dragon for an ancestor.

features.name: Draconic Spells
features.description:
When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

| Sorcerer Level | Spells |
|---|---|
| 3rd | {spell:Alter Self}, {spell:Chromatic Orb}, {spell:Command}, {spell:Dragon's Breath} |
| 5th | {spell:Fear}, {spell:Fly} |
| 7th | {spell:Arcane Eye}, {spell:Charm Monster} |
| 9th | {spell:Legend Lore}, {spell:Summon Dragon} |

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description:
The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic gift. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level.

Parts of you are also covered by dragon-like scales. While you aren't wearing armor, your base {rule:Armor Class} equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.

modifiers.condition: starts at +3 at Sorcerer 3, +1 per additional Sorcerer level

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description:
Your draconic magic has an affinity with a damage type associated with dragons. Choose one of those types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison.

You have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to that damage type, and when you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

properties.text: When you cast a spell dealing damage of your chosen affinity type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them (no action required). For the duration, you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest} unless you spend 3 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

properties.text: Bonus Action: sprout draconic wings for 1 hour (dismissible free). Grants Fly Speed 60 ft. Restore use by spending 3 Sorcery Points.

features.name: Dragon Companion
features.description:
You can cast {spell:Summon Dragon} without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {status:Concentration}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

properties.text: Summon Dragon needs no Material component. Can cast it once per Long Rest without a spell slot. When casting, you may choose to remove Concentration; if so, duration becomes 1 minute.

subclasses.name: Wild Magic Sorcery
subclasses.description: Your innate magic stems from the forces of chaos that underlie the order of creation. You or an ancestor might have endured exposure to raw magic through a planar portal leading to Limbo or the Elemental Planes. Perhaps you were blessed by a fey being or marked by a demon. Or your magic could be a fluke with no apparent cause. Whatever its source, this magic churns within you, waiting for any outlet.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description:
Your spellcasting can unleash surges of untamed magic. Once per turn, you can roll 1d20 immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot. If you roll a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a magical effect.

If the magical effect is a spell, it is too wild to be affected by your Metamagic.

properties.text: Once per turn: after casting a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot, optionally roll 1d20. On a 20, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table (1d100). Resulting spells can't be modified by Metamagic.

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description:
You can manipulate chaos itself to give yourself {rule:Advantage} on one {rule:D20 Test} before you roll the d20. Once you do so, you must cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot or finish a {rule:Long Rest} before you can use this feature again.

If you do cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot before you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you automatically roll on the Wild Magic Surge table.

properties.text: Before rolling a d20, gain Advantage on one D20 Test. Recharges when you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot (but doing so triggers an automatic Wild Magic Surge roll) or finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Bend Luck
features.description: You have the ability to twist fate using your wild magic. Immediately after another creature you can see rolls the d20 for a {rule:D20 Test}, you can take a {rule:Reaction} and spend 1 Sorcery Point to roll 1d4 and apply the number rolled as a bonus or penalty (your choice) to the d20 roll.

properties.text: Reaction: after a creature you can see rolls a D20 Test, spend 1 Sorcery Point to roll 1d4 and add or subtract that value (your choice) from the d20 result.

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: You gain a modicum of control over the surges of your wild magic. Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and use either number.

properties.text: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table (1d100), roll twice and choose which result to use.

features.name: Tamed Surge
features.description:
Immediately after you cast a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot, you can create an effect of your choice from the Wild Magic Surge table instead of rolling on that table. You can choose any effect in the table except for the final row, and if the chosen effect involves a roll, you must make it.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: After casting a Sorcerer spell with a spell slot, choose any Wild Magic Surge table effect (except the final row) instead of rolling. If the chosen effect requires a roll, you still make it. Usable once per Long Rest.
